task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR dashboard, calendar, or main navigation menu is visible.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the Administration > Rules section
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Administration' menu is expanded and 'Rules' (or 'Clinical Decision Rules') is highlighted or clicked.

task_completion.description: The Clinical Decision Rules list is displayed
task_completion.visual_evidence: A page titled 'Clinical Decision Rules' (or similar) is visible, showing a table of rules.

task_completion.description: The 'Adult Weight Screening and Follow-Up' rule is identified
task_completion.visual_evidence: The specific row for 'Adult Weight Screening and Follow-Up' (or ID 'rule_adult_wt_screen_fu') is visible in the viewport.
The checkbox in the 'Active' column for the 'Adult Weight Screening and Follow-Up' row is checked/ticked.

integrity.description: Agent uses the GUI, not terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal, command prompt, or SQL interface is used to manipulate the database directly.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the actual OpenEMR web application
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard OpenEMR web interface (headers, branding, HTML tables), not a text editor or simulation.

integrity.description: Agent does not use browser developer tools to force state
integrity.visual_evidence: Browser developer console is not open or being used to execute scripts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target rule is 'Adult Weight Screening and Follow-Up' (ID: rule_adult_wt_screen_fu). The correct final state requires the 'Active' checkbox for this specific rule to be checked (value 1).
